prompt: Design a Meridian Offline Commerce Intelligence Portal to run inside a retail organization’s on-prem environment for managing product discovery, operational execution, and performance oversight without any internet dependency. Users sign in with a local username and password and see role-based navigation rendered with Thymeleaf server-side pages for fast first paint, with lazy-loaded tables, skeleton screens during long queries, and clear inline validation messages. Merchandisers and Ops Managers use an Efficient Search & Personalized Recommendations experience to locate SKUs using keyword, category, price range (e.g., $10.00–$250.00), condition, post time, and a distance filter driven by a manually entered ZIP code and an offline ZIP-to-mile approximation; the UI provides type-ahead suggestions after 2 characters, a “trending searches” panel computed from the last 7 days of local queries, and a personal search history that can be cleared. Regular Users (store associates) receive in-app notifications for shift check-in windows (e.g., “Check-in opens at 8:30 AM”), upcoming cutoffs (15 minutes prior), missed check-ins, and approval outcomes for exception requests; notifications support user subscriptions by topic, a Do Not Disturb window (default 9:00 PM–7:00 AM), and frequency caps (no more than 3 reminders per event). Analysts and Administrators access KPI dashboards and operations views showing GMV, order volume, conversion rate, average order value, repeat purchase rate, fulfillment timeliness, and cancellation reasons, with multidimensional filtering by time range, product/category/channel, role, and region, plus export to CSV/XLSX and scheduled “report packages” that are generated nightly and saved to a local shared folder for download. The backend uses Spring Boot to expose REST-style endpoints consumed by the Thymeleaf views and to enforce consistent validation, while MySQL persists all business data locally, including orders, products, categories, regions, users/roles, notification templates, subscriptions, recommendations exposures, and analytics events. RBAC is mandatory: Administrators manage roles, templates, and event definitions; Ops Managers approve exceptions; Merchandisers manage discovery rules; Analysts read aggregated data only; Regular Users can view personal tasks and notifications. Authentication uses salted password hashing, a minimum 12-character password policy with lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes, and session timeouts after 30 minutes of inactivity; all state-changing requests include CSRF tokens, server-side output encoding prevents XSS, and APIs reject unknown parameters with strict type/range checks. Sensitive data such as employee identifiers and any stored contact fields are encrypted at rest and masked in the UI (e.g., showing only the last 4 characters). The system implements local-only error monitoring by writing structured logs and alerts into an internal “Health” screen for Administrators, with configurable thresholds (for example, alert when 5xx responses exceed 1% over 15 minutes). Recommendations are generated from locally tracked events (search, view, add-to-cart, purchase) with deduplication (no repeated SKU in a 24-hour window), cold-start fallback to category popularity, and exploration/exploitation controls that guarantee at least 20% long-tail exposure while capping any single SKU’s impressions to 200 per day per region to prevent overexposure.
